title: penstemon eriantherus var. redactus
intro: Penstemon eriantherus var. redactus, also known as the Redstem Beardtongue, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, derived from its leaves and roots. The Redstem Beardtongue has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the Redstem Beardtongue often involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves and roots, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
